Pfizer Digital & Technology Hackathon 2026Preliminary Round
Safe-by-design screening & governance

FoldGate

Screening AI-designed proteins for developability and biosecurity risk, before synthesis.

Team
Northwestern Team #1
Problem statement
Computational pre-clinical drug development
FoldGateNorthwestern Team #1
01 · The problem02 / 08
Problem statement · computational pre-clinical drug development

Choosing which candidates to make is the bottleneck.

AI now designs proteins faster than any lab can build and test them. Over 214 million structures have been predicted, yet most candidates still fail, and not on the science of the target.

Where candidates fail

A newer risk: AI can design proteins that act like known toxins but share almost no sequence, so sequence-based synthesis screening can miss them.

Science, 2025
FoldGateNorthwestern Team #1
02 · The solution03 / 08
Where FoldGate fits

A checkpoint before synthesis.

FoldGate reviews each AI-designed candidate and ranks what is worth making.

Ranks developability and manufacturability
Screens biosecurity by structure, not just sequence
Runs between design and synthesis
AI design→FoldGate→Lab synthesis→Clinic
A therapeutic protein candidate
FoldGateNorthwestern Team #1
03 · Building the MVP04 / 08
How we build it

From a sequence to a defensible decision.

Input
A protein sequence and a predicted 3D structure.
AlphaFoldESMFold
→
Scoring · two models
Developability: aggregation, stability, immunogenicity.
Biosecurity: homology and structural match to known toxins, plus rules. Every hit escalates to a human.
TAPCamSolESM-2ToxinPredVFDB
→
Decision · logged
Pass
Cleared for synthesis
Review
Human expert review
Block
Held back, with reason
Public training data: PDB · UniProt/Swiss-Prot · SAbDab/Thera-SAbDab. Every flag is logged with its reason.
1
Prototype
Both scorers, on public data.
2
Validate
Replay past candidates, tune thresholds.
3
Pilot
Internal assay data, one team.
FoldGateNorthwestern Team #1
04 · Pfizer fit05 / 08
Why Pfizer

Built for Pfizer's pipeline and its values.

Strengthens Stage 1 discovery
Triage across biologics, vaccines and oncology.
De-risks the supply chain early
Weak designs fail before the Supply Sites.
Responsible AI, by design
Audit logs and human review, built in.
Where candidates are made and tested
Developability, not efficacy, drives most biologic attrition. Comms. Biology, 2024
FoldGateNorthwestern Team #1
05 · Non-technical fit06 / 08
Regulatory & commercial

Responsible, and ready to deploy.

Regulatory
Decision-support, not a regulated device. It informs a human gate, it doesn't replace GxP review.
The audit trail supports data-integrity records; biosecurity plugs into existing synthesis-screening norms (IGSC, the US framework).
Commercial
Failing undevelopable candidates early protects real lab and CMC spend before it is committed.
The value is fewer late failures and an auditable record, with no invented savings figure.
Stage 1 discovery

Governance: defensive only, access-controlled, human-in-the-loop. A risk flag with review, never a toxicity dial.

FoldGateNorthwestern Team #1
06 · Roadblocks07 / 08
What stands in the way

The hard parts, answered.

Challenge
Labelled developability data is scarce
→
How we answer it
Scope to antibodies, where public data exists: TAP, the Jain clinical-stage set, Thera-SAbDab. ESM-2 embeddings need few labels.
Challenge
No "danger" labels for biosecurity
→
How we answer it
So we don't train a classifier. Homology and structure matching to known toxins, plus rules. Triage, not an oracle. Every hit goes to a human.
Challenge
False positives could drop good candidates
→
How we answer it
Thresholds tuned on the validation replay. Review and Block both reach a human, so nothing good is dropped silently.
FoldGateNorthwestern Team #1
What changes if this works08 / 08

Fewer dead ends, and a safer shortlist.

Fewer candidates fail late
A safer shortlist into the lab
Review built into the workflow
